description_long: This is a large, roughly pentagonal stone with a flat upper surface and slightly beveled, angular sides. The stone appears to be a single solid piece, with subtle variations in shading that suggest a natural, unpolished texture. Its edges are irregular but not jagged, giving it a somewhat geometric yet organic appearance. The coloration is predominantly gray, with lighter and darker patches that mimic the look of weathered granite or concrete. The surface is matte and hard, with no visible carvings or artificial markings. This stone would be well-suited as a barrier or obstacle in an urban park, plaza, or along a sidewalk.
description: A large, flat-topped, pentagonal gray stone with beveled, irregular sides and a natural, unpolished texture.
description_view_0: Side view showing the elongated, flat top and angular, sloped sides.
description_view_1: Top-down view revealing the pentagonal shape and broad, flat surface.
description_view_2: Opposite side view highlighting the stone's thickness and subtle surface variations.
description_view_3: Bottom-up angled view emphasizing the beveled edges and overall geometric form.
attribute_placement: set as a standalone feature on pavement or soil
attribute_pattern: Subtle natural mottling and veining, irregular but not highly pronounced
attribute_weathering: Slightly weathered with a matte, unpolished surface and minor surface variations
attribute_integration_with_surroundings: Contrasts gently with paved surfaces but can blend with other natural stone elements or landscaping
